summary.intent: Make the gossamer indexer/poll loop more robust by relocating worktree and DB paths into the git root, then extend session indexing to cover shadow branches (in-progress sessions), and fix resulting UI regressions in the sessions list and detail pages.
summary.outcome: Shadow branch indexing works with 4 live sessions tracked across 6 branches; worktree and DB now live under `.gossamer/` in the repo root; session list shows live/checkpoint sessions with intent summaries; session detail refreshes live on new checkpoints and shadow updates; git user/repo name restored in UI.

repo: Shadow branches follow the pattern `entire/<commit-short-hash>-<something>` and contain both `.gossamer/checkpoints/` and `.entire/metadata/<session-id>/` for in-progress sessions.
The same session spans multiple shadow branches as transcripts grow, so deduplication must use a per-session set of blob SHAs (`seenBlobShas` JSON array) rather than a single `fullJsonlBlobSha`.
`getDb()` in `src/db.ts` already calls `mkdirSync(dirname(abs), { recursive: true })` at line 51, so no additional directory creation is needed before DB initialization.
The `entire` tool commits directly to the local `entire/checkpoints/v1` branch ref; `FETCH_HEAD` is stale when SSH remote fetch fails, making `reset --hard FETCH_HEAD` a silent no-op.
FTS (full-text search) tables are virtual tables managed by application code, not the Prisma schema â€” they get rebuilt automatically and cause spurious `data loss` warnings on `prisma db push`.

code.finding: Worktree reset changed from `reset --hard FETCH_HEAD` to `reset --hard ${CHECKPOINT_BRANCH}` â€” uses the local branch ref which is always current when entire commits locally, decoupling correctness from remote SSH availability.
`getGitUser()` helper reads `git config user.name/email`, resolved once at startup relative to `repoDir` and injected into all session responses to restore the GitHub avatar/username in the UI.
`indexAllShadowBranches` reads shadow branch content via `git ls-tree` + `git cat-file blob` directly â€” no worktree needed for shadow branches, only for the committed checkpoint branch.
Detail page was static after initial load; added `subscribeToUpdates` in a second `useEffect` to re-fetch log events and checkpoints on WebSocket broadcast, enabling live updates.
Added `ShadowSession` table with `seenBlobShas String` (JSON array) to track all blob SHAs indexed per session, and optional `shadowSessionId` FK on `LogEvent`.

workflow: When a `replace_all` edit targets a string that appears at multiple indentation levels (e.g., the same key in two different API route handlers), it will miss instances with different leading whitespace â€” each indentation level needs a separate Edit call.
Deleting the DB and running `prisma db push` is the fastest recovery path when schema migrations conflict with virtual/FTS tables that Prisma doesn't manage.
Shadow branch indexing at 5s intervals vs. checkpoint polling at 30s intervals creates a two-tier latency model: live session updates are near-real-time, checkpoint promotion appears within 30s.

friction: `reset --hard FETCH_HEAD` silently did nothing when SSH remote fetch failed, causing the worktree to appear up-to-date (no errors) while actually being stale â€” hard to diagnose.
Prisma `db push` refused to proceed due to FTS virtual tables it didn't create, requiring `--force-reset` / manual DB deletion to recover.
Two `replace_all` edits for `gitUserName: null` and `repoName: null` each missed a second occurrence in the `:id` endpoint due to differing indentation, requiring a follow-up fix each time.
The old worktree registered at `/private/tmp/gossamer-checkpoints` blocked creation of the new `.gossamer/checkpoints` worktree and had to be manually pruned before the server could start.
open_items: New checkpoints appear in session detail within at most 30 seconds (the checkpoint poll interval) â€” could be reduced or made event-driven if lower latency is needed.
Shadow branch indexing uses `git branch` on the worktree which only sees local branches; remote-only shadow branches from other machines would be missed unless a fetch succeeds.
The legacy `Session` table is no longer consulted for session listing but may still exist in the DB â€” no migration or cleanup was done to remove stale rows.
SSH key availability for remote fetch is silently swallowed; if the remote diverges from the local branch (e.g., force-push), the worktree will never update.
